# americancloud_ssh_key (Resource)

An SSH key pair. Provide `public_key` to register an existing key, or omit it to have one generated — the `private_key` is then returned once and stored in state.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Register an existing public key
resource "americancloud_ssh_key" "laptop" {
  name       = "my-laptop"
  public_key = "ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAI... user@host"
}

# Or omit public_key to have a pair generated (private_key is returned once)
resource "americancloud_ssh_key" "generated" {
  name = "ci-deploy"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Name of the SSH key pair. Changing this forces a new key.

### Optional

- `public_key` (String) Public key in OpenSSH format. Omit to generate a new pair. Changing it forces a new key.

### Read-Only

- `fingerprint` (String) Fingerprint of the key.
- `id` (String) Key identifier (the key name).
- `private_key` (String, Sensitive) Generated private key — set only when `public_key` is omitted, returned once at creation.
